artPrompt: Two goblins squared off across a folding table in the firelit square, fists thrust forward mid-throw in furious rock-paper-scissors, mouths open in matching yells, onlookers blurring around them; bonfire glow washes flame-orange over their straining faces, sparks and confetti swirling through ember-red haze and night-black smoke, the showdown frozen in a dreamy, tender blur, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: Two Out of Three (Best Of)
text:
Challenge target opponent to rock-paper-scissors, best of three. The winner is determined the way it always is, by yelling and pointing.
If you win, Two Out of Three deals X damage to that opponent and you draw a card.
If you lose, it deals X damage to you, but you may demand best of five. They are not required to agree. They will agree, out of pity.
flavorText: Whateverville settles all disputes this way. The courthouse is just a folding table.
